title: The One Where No One's Ready

jokes.text: Yeah, it's fat. I drank fat!
jokes.setup: Character tastes mysterious liquid thinking it might be cider
jokes.punchline: Discovers he drank pure fat and reacts with disgust
jokes.explanation: Good gross-out comedy with Joey's characteristic food-related mishaps. Short and punchy delivery.

jokes.text: We have to be out the door at 7:40. / We have a half-hour. / That was four minutes ago.
jokes.setup: Ross anxiously tracking time while others are relaxed about getting ready
jokes.punchline: Reveals they've already lost time while discussing having time
jokes.explanation: Classic Ross neurotic behavior establishing the episode's time pressure premise.

jokes.text: How come you didn't come over earlier? / Because I'm a stupid, stupid man.
jokes.setup: Monica asks reasonable question about timing
jokes.punchline: Ross's overly harsh self-criticism
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment showing Ross's tendency toward self-flagellation.

jokes.text: And that takes, what? Just 6 or 7 minutes? / Yeah. Once I figure out what I'm wearing.
jokes.setup: Ross assumes getting dressed is quick
jokes.punchline: Rachel reveals the real time-consuming part is decision-making
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff revealing the gendered difference in getting ready.

jokes.text: Glass of fat?
jokes.setup: Joey offering the same disgusting liquid he just discovered
jokes.punchline: Casually offering something he knows is revolting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey logic - offering disgusting thing as if it's normal. Great callback with economy.

jokes.text: You wanna hear it? / Am I in it? / After I thank everyone for donating to the museum... I sing a song about the wonder that is Joey.
jokes.setup: Ross offers to share his speech, Joey asks if he's mentioned
jokes.punchline: Ross sarcastically suggests an absurd Joey-centric speech
jokes.explanation: Good character interplay showing Joey's self-centeredness and Ross's sarcasm.

jokes.text: Thank you. I know, though.
jokes.setup: Someone compliments Phoebe's appearance
jokes.punchline: Phoebe matter-of-factly agrees with the compliment
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe lack of false modesty delivered with perfect timing.

jokes.text: All right, I took the quiz. And it turns out I do put career before men.
jokes.setup: Monica randomly announces quiz results
jokes.punchline: Admits to stereotypical behavior pattern
jokes.explanation: Mild character insight but not particularly surprising or funny.

jokes.text: Well, it's not like I went to Spain.
jokes.setup: Arguing about bathroom break being too long to hold a seat
jokes.punchline: Chandler's absurd comparison to international travel
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Chandler logic with great quotability and economy.

jokes.text: Well, actually, the last place you were sitting was in there, so...
jokes.setup: Chandler claims the chair because he was sitting there last
jokes.punchline: Joey points out Chandler was literally sitting on the toilet
jokes.explanation: Clever literal interpretation that undercuts Chandler's argument.

jokes.text: Are you gonna do magic?
jokes.setup: Joey sees Phoebe in formal attire
jokes.punchline: Assumes she's dressed as a magician
jokes.explanation: Joey's literal interpretation of formal wear, though somewhat obvious.

jokes.text: That's funny. Change.
jokes.setup: Joey makes magic comment
jokes.punchline: Ross's blunt dismissal with no humor acknowledgment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross impatience - acknowledges joke but immediately dismisses it.

jokes.text: He could not, would not, want to wait.
jokes.setup: Ross expressing urgency about time
jokes.punchline: Joey responds in Dr. Seuss style
jokes.explanation: Clever literary reference that's unexpected from Joey, well-executed wordplay.

jokes.text: I don't know. You might be the first one.
jokes.setup: Rachel asks if her outfit looks like something a paleontologist's girlfriend would wear
jokes.punchline: Chandler points out the lack of precedent
jokes.explanation: Smart observational humor about the specificity of Rachel's concern.

jokes.text: Well, Joey, I wrote a little song today. It's called, 'Get up!'
jokes.setup: Ross's mounting frustration with Joey in the chair
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically announces minimalist 'song' title
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Ross's frustration with creative passive-aggressive approach.

jokes.text: It's me, I'm coming over. What if I'm already there when you play this message? Is that too spooky?
jokes.setup: Chandler leaving an answering machine message
jokes.punchline: Philosophical question about temporal paradox of messages
jokes.explanation: Genuinely clever meta-humor about answering machine logistics with Chandler's voice.

jokes.text: You know what I did? I got dressed.
jokes.setup: Ross comparing his breakup experience to Monica's current situation
jokes.punchline: Anticlimatic advice that's hilariously practical
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross moment - takes emotional situation and reduces it to practical logistics.

jokes.text: Not touching. Can't get mad! Not touching. Can't get mad!
jokes.setup: Chandler trying to reclaim his chair while Joey sits in it
jokes.punchline: Childish logic about harassment rules
jokes.explanation: Classic physical comedy escalation with schoolyard logic.

jokes.text: You rotten boys!
jokes.setup: Chandler and Joey's chair fight gets Phoebe's dress dirty
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's old-fashioned scolding language
jokes.explanation: Mildly amusing character-specific language but not particularly strong.

jokes.text: Maybe he called to say you're obsessive and crazy?
jokes.setup: Monica obsessing over Richard's phone message
jokes.punchline: Chandler's blunt assessment of her behavior
jokes.explanation: Good Chandler directness cutting through Monica's overthinking.

jokes.text: I am so comfortable. / Me too. In fact, I think I might be a little too comfortable.
jokes.setup: Both guys sitting uncomfortably close in the same chair
jokes.punchline: Chandler's suggestive comment about their proximity
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the chair situation with mild sexual tension humor.

jokes.text: When I get back, it's Chair City. And I'm the guy... who's sitting in a chair!
jokes.setup: Chandler leaving to change clothes with chair agreement
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top declaration about chair ownership
jokes.explanation: Chandler's theatrical declaration is funny but slightly overwritten.

jokes.text: Not 'out of that.' Not 'out' of clothes.
jokes.setup: Monica suggesting Phoebe get out of stained dress
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's fear of undressing in front of others
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay on phrasing but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: How could it not be breezy? No. Because you're in such a breezy place.
jokes.setup: Monica worried her phone message wasn't casual enough
jokes.punchline: Rachel's sarcastic assessment of Monica's current mental state
jokes.explanation: Good sarcasm highlighting the irony of Monica's anxious state.

jokes.text: What a cool job. 'You have two new messages.' Please pass the pie.
jokes.setup: Answering machine announces message count
jokes.punchline: Joey's random admiration for the job and non-sequitur
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey non-sequitur thinking with his unique perspective on mundane things.

jokes.text: You can't say you're 'breezy.' That negates the 'breezy.'
jokes.setup: Monica ending her message by declaring 'I'm breezy!'
jokes.punchline: Rachel pointing out the logical contradiction
jokes.explanation: Excellent observational humor about self-awareness defeating its own purpose. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Now, she sounded breezy.
jokes.setup: Hearing another woman's casual message to Richard
jokes.punchline: Monica's bitter acknowledgment of actual breeziness
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the breezy theme with Monica's jealous observation.

jokes.text: So in the words of A.A. Milne, 'Get out of my chair, dill-hole.'
jokes.setup: Chandler trying to sound literary and sophisticated
jokes.punchline: Attributing crude insult to Winnie the Pooh author
jokes.explanation: Brilliant juxtaposition of highbrow literary reference with crude insult. Highly quotable and perfectly Chandler.

jokes.text: That's right! I'm taking the essence.
jokes.setup: Chandler removes chair cushions as retaliation
jokes.punchline: Philosophical declaration about chair's true nature
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with pseudo-philosophical justification for petty revenge.

jokes.text: I didn't recognize it without that inflatable sheep.
jokes.setup: Someone wearing Halloween costume inappropriately
jokes.punchline: Reference to costume's missing sexual prop
jokes.explanation: Unexpected sexual reference from Ross with good visual comedy setup.

jokes.text: Yeah, which, by the way, Chandler, I would like back one of these days.
jokes.setup: Ross mentions inflatable sheep
jokes.punchline: Rachel casually requests return of sex toy
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up extending the uncomfortable sexual reference.

jokes.text: Hey, what you do on your own time...
jokes.setup: Chandler mentions using sheep as camping pillows
jokes.punchline: Ross's suggestive misinterpretation
jokes.explanation: Standard sexual innuendo but well-timed as response.

jokes.text: Because I'm not wearing any underwear now.
jokes.setup: Question about why Joey needs underwear for tux
jokes.punchline: Joey's casual admission of current commando status
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey oversharing with perfect matter-of-fact delivery.

jokes.text: I'm not gonna go commando in another man's fatigues.
jokes.setup: Explaining reluctance to go underwear-less in rental tux
jokes.punchline: Military terminology for formal wear situation
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay mixing military and formal wear terminology. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: What, are you gonna show me my clothes? / Hey, opposite... is opposite. / He's got nothing!
jokes.setup: Chandler threatens to do opposite of hiding clothes
jokes.punchline: Chandler's failed logic and Joey's triumphant realization
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing both characters' flawed logic and Joey's rare moment of clarity.

jokes.text: What are you supporting? / Christmas.
jokes.setup: Phoebe wearing ribbon as political statement
jokes.punchline: Supporting universally celebrated holiday
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe logic - being political about something completely non-controversial. Great economy.

jokes.text: What time is it? It's 7:33. I have 7 minutes. I have seven minutes!
jokes.setup: Ross realizes his watch stopped
jokes.punchline: Mounting panic with repetitive emphasis
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ross's anxiety but somewhat repetitive.

jokes.text: Yeah, pants! What an idea! Or better yet, go without pants.
jokes.setup: Rachel considering changing to pants
jokes.punchline: Ross's sarcastic escalation to absurdity
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Ross's frustration with sarcastic suggestion.

jokes.text: Donald Duck never wore pants. But when he gets out of the shower... he always puts a towel around his waist. I mean, what is that about?
jokes.setup: Awkward silence during phone call
jokes.punchline: Random philosophical observation about cartoon logic
jokes.explanation: Brilliant observational humor about cartoon inconsistency. Became iconic and highly quotable. Perfect awkward conversation filler.

jokes.text: That bitch always hated me.
jokes.setup: Monica getting increasingly upset about Michelle
jokes.punchline: Sudden harsh assessment after friendly conversation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Monica's jealous spiral but fairly standard.

jokes.text: I'm not gonna go. / You're not gonna go? / No, I think I'm gonna catch up on my correspondence.
jokes.setup: Phoebe announcing she won't attend the event
jokes.punchline: Old-fashioned excuse for staying home
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe old-fashioned language contrasting with modern situation.

jokes.text: So I think that will accomplish the 'not going.'
jokes.setup: Ross asking how she can not go
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's literal explanation of not going
jokes.explanation: Good Phoebe literal-mindedness with circular logic.

jokes.text: I'm just, I don't know, not in a very museum benefit-y kind of mood.
jokes.setup: Phoebe explaining why she doesn't want to go
jokes.punchline: Made-up adjective describing specific emotional state
jokes.explanation: Charming Phoebe neologism creating specific mood descriptor.

jokes.text: Is it wrong that I was totally aroused by that?
jokes.setup: Ross aggressively yelling at Monica about phone
jokes.punchline: Chandler's inappropriate sexual response to aggression
jokes.explanation: Typical Chandler inappropriate sexual humor, well-timed but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Look at me! I'm Chandler. Could I be wearing any more clothes?
jokes.setup: Joey wearing all of Chandler's clothes as retaliation
jokes.punchline: Parody of Chandler's speech pattern while ridiculous outfit
jokes.explanation: Iconic moment perfectly capturing Chandler's speech pattern while escalating physical comedy. Became cultural touchstone and endlessly quoted.

jokes.text: I better not do any, I don't know, lunges.
jokes.setup: Joey overheating in multiple layers of clothes
jokes.punchline: Specific exercise that would be problematic
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to clothes joke with specific physical comedy concern.

jokes.text: You could drink the fat.
jokes.setup: Ross asking what he can do to show he wants Rachel there
jokes.punchline: Reference back to the disgusting drink from opening
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback tying to episode opening with perfect timing and escalation.

jokes.text: Okay, vanilla milkshake. Just a vanilla milkshake... with chicken bits floating in it.
jokes.setup: Ross psyching himself up to drink the fat
jokes.punchline: Trying to make it sound appealing then adding disgusting detail
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of disgust with Ross's attempt at positive self-talk.

jokes.text: Let's see what else he'll do!
jokes.setup: Ross about to drink fat to prove his dedication
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's excited suggestion to keep testing him
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Phoebe's mischievous side.

jokes.text: Maybe I'm getting my period or something. I don't know.
jokes.setup: Monica leaving message explaining her crazy behavior
jokes.punchline: Awkward oversharing about menstrual cycle
jokes.explanation: Standard oversharing humor typical of the show's era but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: Oh, and by the way... What? I'm going commando too.
jokes.setup: Everyone finally ready to leave
jokes.punchline: Rachel's casual admission matching Joey's situation
jokes.explanation: Good callback to underwear theme with unexpected character participation.

jokes.text: All right, that's it. Give me your underwear.
jokes.setup: Chair argument continuing at the museum event
jokes.punchline: Ross applying the episode's underwear-stealing solution to new situation
jokes.explanation: Excellent episode-ending callback combining multiple episode themes. Ross applying the apartment logic to formal setting.

tmdb_overview: In a race against the clock, a stressed Ross tries to get the gang dressed and ready for a black-tie benefit at the museum while Joey and Chandler clash over personal belongings and personal space.
